preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [ae] to the close-mid front unrounded vowel [e] is a vowel merger process documented across multiple language families. This represents the reduction of a diphthong with distinct glide and vocalic components into a single vowel segment.

examples.note: Historical monophthongization of diphthongal sequences in the transition from Old English to Middle English.
Monophthongization of Latin diphthong [ae] to [e] in the development of Romance languages.
Diphthong reduction in certain German dialects, particularly in southern regions.
Historical monophthongization in Old Swedish → Middle Swedish evolution.
